Title: Late embryogenesis abundant gene LEA3 (Gh_A08G0694) enhances drought and salt stress tolerance in cotton

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Gh_A08G0694 is also referred to as GhLEA3, belongs to the group 3 LEA protein family, and is located on chromosome A08 where multiple LEA3 genes cluster. Among 78 cotton LEA3 genes identified, Gh_A08G0694 showed the strongest induction in leaves and roots under drought and salt stress and was therefore chosen as the key candidate gene for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. A genome-wide survey identified 78 LEA3 genes in cotton, and Gh_A08G0694 (GhLEA3) on chromosome A08 was the most highly induced gene in leaves and roots under drought and salt stress.
2. Gh_A08G0694 protein is localized to chloroplasts and belongs to the group 3 LEA family containing the LEA3 Pfam domain PF03242.
3. VIGS knockdown of Gh_A08G0694 in cotton causes heightened sensitivity to drought and salinity, with reduced biomass, chlorophyll content, relative leaf water content, and antioxidant activity, and increased ion leakage, excised leaf water loss, H2O2 and MDA accumulation; expression of stress-related genes GhP5CS, GhMYB and GhGT-2 is reduced under stress.
4. Overexpression of Gh_A08G0694 in Arabidopsis enhances drought and salt tolerance, increasing survival after rewatering, improving water status, lowering ion leakage and oxidant levels, increasing antioxidant enzyme activities, and promoting higher germination rates and longer roots under NaCl and mannitol; stress-responsive genes AB15, P5CS and RD22 are upregulated.
5. Yeast two-hybrid assays show that Gh_A08G0694 directly interacts with GhVDAC1 and GhGAPA/gapA, and silencing Gh_A08G0694 downregulates VDAC1 and GAPA expression, indicating that Gh_A08G0694 positively regulates these organelle-associated stress-related genes to enhance osmotic stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of drought and salt stress tolerance: knockdown in cotton increases sensitivity to osmotic and salt stress, while overexpression (in Arabidopsis) enhances survival, water retention, membrane stability, antioxidant capacity and root growth under drought and salinity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a protective LEA3 protein localized in chloroplasts and interacting with mitochondrial outer membrane protein VDAC1 and chloroplastic GAPA, contributing to stabilization of organelle function, maintenance of ROS balance and modulation of downstream stress-responsive gene expression during drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA- and osmotic-stress-responsive pathways that involve calcium signaling via VDAC1 and metabolic energy supply via GAPA, integrating these processes to enhance cotton tolerance to drought and salinity.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation:
Gh_A08G0694 --(activation)--> GhP5CS, GhMYB and GhGT-2 in cotton under drought and salt stress, as their transcript levels are significantly reduced in Gh_A08G0694-silenced plants compared with wild type.
Gh_A08G0694 --(activation)--> AB15, P5CS and RD22 in Arabidopsis, as these stress-responsive genes are more highly expressed in Gh_A08G0694-overexpressing lines than in wild-type plants under stress.
Gh_A08G0694 --(activation)--> GhVDAC1 and GhGAPA/gapA, as their expression is downregulated in Gh_A08G0694-silenced cotton plants under drought stress relative to wild type.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group 3 late embryogenesis abundant (LEA3) protein containing the LEA3 Pfam conserved domain PF03242.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast localization confirmed by eGFP-Gh_A08G0694 fusion protein transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and observed as clustered chloroplastic GFP signal.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhVDAC1 (Gh_A05G2633) and GhGAPA/gapA (Gh_D10G0782) directly interact with Gh_A08G0694 as shown by yeast two-hybrid assays, whereas GhSRC2 and GhDnaJ showed no interaction.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Gh_A08G0694 (GhLEA3) is strongly induced in cotton roots and leaves from 3 h to 48 h after exposure to drought (PEG-6000) and salt (NaCl) stress, with much higher expression in stressed tissues than under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought simulated with 17% PEG-6000 and by withholding water; salinity imposed with 250 mmol·L^-1 NaCl in hydroponic solution and soil; additional osmotic and salt stress applied to Arabidopsis seeds and seedlings using mannitol (100–300 mmol·L^-1) and NaCl (75–150 mmol·L^-1).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and improvement of drought and salinity tolerance in cotton through functional characterization of a LEA3 gene.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salt/osmotic stress)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Knockdown plants display reduced chlorophyll content, reduced root and shoot fresh weight, reduced relative leaf water content, increased excised leaf water loss, and increased ion leakage, together with reduced POD activity and increased H2O2 and MDA accumulation under stress. Overexpressing lines show lower ion leakage and excised leaf water loss, higher relative leaf water content, higher CAT and POD activities, lower H2O2 and MDA levels, higher germination rates, and longer roots under NaCl and mannitol stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Gossypium hirsutum cv. Marie-Galante 85 (MG-85) seedlings; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia-0 (Col-0) transgenic lines overexpressing Gh_A08G0694; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient eGFP localization; Y2HGold yeast cells carrying pGBKT7-Gh_A08G0694 and pGADT7-based prey constructs; VIGS constructs pTRV2:Gh_A08G0694 and pTRV2:PDS.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of LEA3 genes using Pfam domain PF03242; phylogenetic and motif analysis; promoter cis-element and GO analysis; RT-qPCR expression profiling under drought and salt stress; VIGS in cotton; construction of overexpression vector PBI121-Gh_A08G0694 and transformation of Arabidopsis; drought stress by water withholding and PEG-6000, salinity stress by NaCl, and osmotic stress by mannitol; measurements of chlorophyll content, ion leakage, excised leaf water loss, relative leaf water content, plant height, root length and biomass; biochemical assays for CAT, POD, H2O2 and MDA; subcellular localization of eGFP-Gh_A08G0694 in N. benthamiana; yeast two-hybrid library screening and validation; GO and KEGG pathway enrichment of interactors; germination and root elongation assays under NaCl and mannitol.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS) knockdown in cotton; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana; physiological and biochemical stress assays; RT-qPCR of stress-responsive genes; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays; subcellular localization of eGFP-Gh_A08G0694.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: LEA3 family members were identified in cotton genomes using the LEA3 Pfam domain PF03242; expression profiling under drought and salt stress highlighted Gh_A08G0694 as the most highly induced LEA3 gene in roots and leaves; its CDS was amplified from G. hirsutum MG-85 cDNA and cloned into VIGS and overexpression vectors for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Provides a strong candidate gene for breeding or engineering cotton with improved drought and salinity tolerance; overexpression of Gh_A08G0694 is proposed to enhance osmotic stress tolerance, stabilize organelle function and energy supply, and thereby improve yield stability under variable, stress-prone environments.
